commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Ozaziu does not occur before. Septuagint have Ozias both here and ver. 18, after Jehiel. (Haydock)
Of victory, such as was customary on the like occasions.
For the octave. Hebrew hasheminith, according to the Jews, means an instrument of eight strings; so when ten is mentioned, the Septuagint frequently supply "strings "Psalm xxxii. 2. (Menochius)
Hebrew, "they had guitars, and presided over the eighth band to intone. All the bands formed four choirs, distinguished by their instruments, the cymbal, nable, cinnor, (or guitar) and trumpets. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
